senses.definition: a makeshift or rough repair or device, especially one made quickly and crudely
examples: He used a temporary bodge to fix the leak.
The car’s repair was a clever bodge to get it running again.

senses.definition: a member of a criminal or violent street gang in some parts of Australia; often used to describe a delinquent youth
examples: The teenagers were accused of acting like bodgies.
The gang known as bodgies frequented the area in the 1950s.

senses.definition: more of a bodge; more crude or makeshift in quality
examples: A bodgier repair won’t last long.
That bodgier solution is plainly temporary.

senses.definition: plural of bodgie; members of a youth gang known for rebellious behavior
examples: The bodgies lounged near the arcade.
Historical accounts describe bodgies as part of 1950s street culture.

senses.definition: most crude or makeshift in quality
examples: That’s the bodgiest fix I’ve ever seen.
We might need to redo the bodgiest part of the project.

senses.definition: to make or repair something in a makeshift or crude way
examples: She was bodging together a shelf from leftover wood.
They spent the afternoon bodging a temporary fix.

senses.definition: the sacred fig tree or its fig leaves, under which the Buddha attained enlightenment; a symbolic term for enlightenment in Buddhism
examples: Bodhi represents awakening in Buddhist tradition.
Pilgrims visit the Bodhi tree in Bodh Gaya.

senses.definition: a shortened form of bodhisattva; a being on the path to Buddhahood who delays enlightenment to help others
examples: The teacher spoke about the bodhisat’s vow to aid beings.
In practice, a bodhisat works for the benefit of all.

senses.definition: a being who seeks enlightenment for the good of all beings, often delaying final nirvana to help others
examples: A compassionate bodhisattva delays their own Enlightenment to help others.
The sutras describe many bodhisattvas with great wisdom.

senses.definition: plural of bodhisattva; multiple enlightened beings who help others
examples: Many bodhisattvas are venerated in Mahayana traditions.
The temple has statues of several bodhisattvas.

senses.definition: an alternate transliteration of bodhisattva; a being on the path to Buddhahood
examples: The scripture discusses the bodhisattwa’s compassionate activity.

senses.definition: a traditional Irish drum played with a tipper, used in folk music
examples: The musician tapped the bodhran to the beat.
They learned a jig on the bodhran.

senses.definition: plural of bodhran; traditional Irish drums
examples: The bodhrans accompanied the flute.
Several bodhrans were used in the ensemble.

senses.definition: the part of a woman's dress covering the chest and back; a close-fitting upper part of a dress
examples: She wore a satin bodice with a lace overlay.
The costume featured a corseted bodice.

senses.definition: having a bodice or a fitted upper garment
examples: A bodiced dress was displayed at the boutique.
She wore a bodiced gown for the gala.

senses.definition: a person or device that makes or shapes a bodice or similar garment; largely historical or specialized term
examples: The tailor acted as a bodicemaker for the period costume.

senses.definition: the craft of making bodices or shaping upper garments; often historical term
examples: Historical bodicemaking required skilled sewing.

senses.definition: plural of bodice; multiple upper garment pieces for the chest area
examples: The dresses had strapless bodices.
She altered the bodices of the gowns.

senses.definition: having a body; formed or filled with something
examples: The forest was well-bodied with trees.
A well-bodied organism grows steadily.

senses.definition: one who bodies; a person with a lot of body (informal) or comparative form of body-related meaning
examples: He’s a serious bodier in the gym context.

senses.definition: a playful or informal term; possibly a diminutive for someone named Bodie or similar; not a standard dictionary entry
examples: I called my friend Bodieron as a joke.

senses.definition: the physical bodies of living beings; plural of body
examples: Several bodies were found after the incident.
The human bodies were laid out for autopsy.

senses.definition: a small, often decorative brass or metal trinket; sometimes used as a colloquial term for a small person or thing
examples: She kept a tiny bodikin on the shelf.

senses.definition: a term of endearment for a child or small person; diminutive of bodkin in playful use
examples: Come here, bodikins!

senses.definition: without a body; incorporeal or lacking a physical form
examples: The ghost was bodiless and could pass through walls.
A bodiless voice spoke from the darkness.
